assertions.with_skill_evidence: Created `pub struct Email(String)` with validated `Email::new()` constructor that returns Result
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No newtype created; send_email still accepts bare &str

assertions.with_skill_evidence: ConfigBuilder<Pending>/ConfigBuilder<Ready> with PhantomData; Connection<Disconnected>/Connected/Authenticated with compile-time state transitions
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Connection<S> with Disconnected/Connected/Authenticated states enforcing compile-time transitions

assertions.with_skill_evidence: send_email takes &str for body parameter
assertions.without_skill_evidence: send_email changed to accept &str instead of &String

assertions.with_skill_evidence: host(self, host: impl Into<String>) uses impl Trait for flexible args
assertions.without_skill_evidence: fn host(mut self, host: impl Into<String>) uses impl Trait

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Setters consume self and return Self/ConfigBuilder<Ready>; build() returns Config (though not Result, it's valid since typestate guarantees validity)
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Builder methods take owned self, return Self for chaining. No build() method though — Config IS the builder, no separate build step returning Result
assertions.without_skill_note: Borderline — setters consume and return Self, but there's no build() method that returns Result. Grading PASS since the consume-and-return-Self pattern is present.

assertions.with_skill_evidence: mod private { pub trait Sealed {} } and pub trait Plugin: private::Sealed
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Added Send + Sync supertraits but no sealed trait pattern; Plugin is still open to external impl

assertions.with_skill_evidence: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)] on Point; distance() takes Point by value
assertions.without_skill_evidence: #[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)] on Point; distance takes by value

assertions.with_skill_evidence: No use of Cow<'_, T> anywhere in the code, and no mention of it
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No use of Cow<'_, T> anywhere in the code, and no mention of it

assertions.with_skill_evidence: No use of Cell/RefCell; still uses Arc<Mutex> for TreeNode without noting interior mutability alternatives
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No use of Cell/RefCell; still uses Arc<Mutex> without noting interior mutability alternatives

assertions.with_skill_evidence: parent: Option<Weak<Mutex<TreeNode>>> with comment 'Weak breaks the cycle'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: parent: Option<Weak<Mutex<TreeNode>>> with comment 'Weak breaks the cycle'

assertions.with_skill_evidence: No mention of bumpalo or arena pattern as alternative to Arc<Mutex<TreeNode>>
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of bumpalo or arena pattern

assertions.with_skill_evidence: left: Box<Expr>, right: Box<Expr> in BinaryOp variant; also Box<str> for op
assertions.without_skill_evidence: left: Box<Expr>, right: Box<Expr> in BinaryOp; also Box<str> for op

assertions.with_skill_evidence: DbError uses #[derive(Debug, Error)] from thiserror; load_user returns anyhow::Result with Context
assertions.without_skill_evidence: AppError uses #[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]; manual DbError for library. However anyhow is not used — thiserror used for both lib and app layers. Grading PASS since thiserror is used for structured errors (the key intent).
assertions.without_skill_note: Borderline — no anyhow usage, but thiserror is correctly applied. The assertion says 'thiserror for lib, anyhow for application'. Without-skill uses thiserror for both. Changing to FAIL for strict grading.

assertions.with_skill_evidence: .context('failed to load user {id}'), .context('failed to read config.toml'), .context('failed to parse config.toml') on ? calls
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Uses .map_err() to wrap errors into AppError variants, but no .context() or .with_context() from anyhow

assertions.with_skill_evidence: No .unwrap() calls; errors propagated with ? and .context()
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No .unwrap() calls; tokio::sync::Mutex::lock is infallible, errors propagated with ? and .map_err()

assertions.with_skill_evidence: maybe_fail() now propagates with do_something_important()? instead of let _ =
assertions.without_skill_evidence: maybe_fail() propagates with do_something_important()? instead of let _ =

assertions.with_skill_evidence: tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || heavy_computation(&item)) offloads CPU work
assertions.without_skill_evidence: tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || heavy_computation(item)) offloads CPU work

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Switched to tokio::sync::Mutex with comment 'tokio::sync::Mutex instead of std::sync::Mutex — safe across .await'
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Switched to tokio::sync::Mutex with comment 'tokio::sync::Mutex is .await-safe'; mentioned !Send futures in summary

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Guard scoped in block: let count = { let guard = results.lock().await; guard.len() }; then some_async_operation().await after guard dropped
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Guard scoped in block: let count = { let guard = results.lock().await; guard.len() }; guard dropped before .await

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Still uses Arc<Mutex<Vec>> for results collection; no channels (mpsc/broadcast) used
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Still uses Arc<Mutex<Vec>> for results collection; no channels used

assertions.with_skill_evidence: No mention of cancellation safety or cleanup on drop
assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of cancellation safety or cleanup on drop

assertions.without_skill_evidence: tracing::info!(count, 'batch processing complete') replaces println!

assertions.without_skill_evidence: HashMap::with_capacity(entries.len()) in build_index; collect() uses size_hint internally

assertions.with_skill_evidence: ids.iter().map(|id| format!('id-{id}')).collect() replaces manual for loop
assertions.without_skill_evidence: ids.iter().map(|id| format!('id-{}', id)).collect() replaces manual loop

assertions.without_skill_evidence: EventQueue uses VecDeque<Event> replacing LinkedList with cache locality explanation

assertions.without_skill_evidence: Fix B shows enum dispatch to eliminate dynamic dispatch; mentions generics as alternative

assertions.with_skill_evidence: Recommends lto = 'fat' and codegen-units = 1 in [profile.release]
assertions.without_skill_evidence: Recommends lto = 'fat', codegen-units = 1, panic = 'abort' in [profile.release]

assertions.without_skill_evidence: No mention of ahash, fxhash, rustc_hash, or any alternative hasher

assertions.with_skill_evidence: static GLOBAL_CONFIG: LazyLock<Mutex<Config>> = LazyLock::new(...) replacing lazy_static!
assertions.without_skill_evidence: OnceLock used via static INSTANCE: OnceLock<Mutex<Config>> = OnceLock::new() replacing lazy_static!

assertions.without_skill_evidence: Matrix uses runtime rows/cols fields with flat Vec<f64>; no const generics

assertions.with_skill_evidence: a.zip(b).map(|(x, y)| x + y) replaces nested match — uses combinators
assertions.without_skill_evidence: a.zip(b).map(|(x, y)| x + y) replaces nested match — uses combinators

assertions.with_skill_evidence: fn read_config(path: impl AsRef<Path>) accepts String, &str, PathBuf, Path
assertions.without_skill_evidence: fn read_config(path: &Path) — changed from PathBuf to &Path. Not impl AsRef<Path> but still accepts borrowed path. Grading FAIL for strict interpretation since assertion says 'impl AsRef<Path>'.
assertions.without_skill_note: Changed to &Path instead of impl AsRef<Path>. While better than PathBuf, it doesn't match the assertion requirement.
